title: Distance-Aware Flow Matching for LiDAR Point Cloud Generation
description: We propose DistFlow, a flow matching framework that incorporates radial distance awareness to address the inherent spatial imbalance in LiDAR point clouds. By weighting the flow matching objective based on distance from the sensor, we improve generation quality for distant sparse regions while maintaining near-field fidelity.
motivation: LiDAR point clouds exhibit severe distance-dependent density variation: near-field (0-20m) contains 60-70% of points while far-field (50m+) contains only 5-10%. Standard flow matching treats all points equally, causing underfitting of distant structures that are critical for autonomous driving safety. Existing adaptive timestep methods (Tariq et al., 2026) address temporal sampling but not this spatial imbalance.
proposed_approach: DistFlow introduces three key innovations: (1) Radial distance-weighted flow loss that upweights gradients from distant points using inverse distance weighting (IDW) with learnable α and β parameters, or a learned adaptive weighting (LAW) variant using an 8K-parameter MLP operating on local density features; (2) A lightweight Point Transformer (~4.2M params) with distance-conditioned FiLM embeddings that incorporate radial distance statistics into timestep conditioning; (3) Multi-scale distance stratification that balances sampling across near/mid/far field regions during training.

related_work.paper: Adaptive Time Step Flow Matching for Autonomous Driving Motion Planning (Tariq et al., 2026)
related_work.difference: They learn a variance estimator for adaptive timestep selection in motion planning; we address spatial sampling imbalance for point cloud generation. The problems are orthogonal and complementary.

related_work.paper: Flow Matching for Generative Modeling (Lipman et al., 2023)
related_work.difference: We extend the standard flow matching objective with distance-aware weighting specifically designed for LiDAR geometry.

related_work.paper: RAP: Registration via Adaptive Point Flow (Sun et al., 2025)
related_work.difference: They apply flow matching to point cloud registration (pose estimation); we target generative modeling from noise.

related_work.paper: DynamicCity: Large-scale 4D Occupancy Generation (Bian et al., 2024)
related_work.difference: They use latent flow matching for occupancy forecasting; we focus on raw point generation with explicit distance-aware training.

related_work.paper: Curriculum Sampling for Efficient Training of Flow Matching (Sun, P., 2026)
related_work.difference: They adapt timestep sampling distribution during training; we adapt spatial sampling based on radial distance.

related_work.paper: LiFlow: Flow Matching for 3D LiDAR Scene Completion (Matteazzi & Tutsch, 2026)
related_work.difference: LiFlow is a concurrent work (Feb 2026) that applies flow matching to conditional scene completion (incomplete scan → complete scan) using nearest-neighbor flow matching. DistFlow addresses unconditional generation (noise → realistic scan) with radial distance-aware loss weighting. The problems are fundamentally different: completion requires paired input-output data and establishes point-wise correspondences; unconditional generation samples from a learned distribution and must address spatial density imbalance. The techniques are complementary—LiFlow's architecture could benefit from DistFlow's distance weighting.

related_work.paper: Improving Progressive Generation with Decomposable Flow Matching (Haji-Ali et al., 2025)
related_work.difference: They apply flow matching independently at each level of a multi-scale spectral decomposition (Laplacian pyramid) for images/videos. We use radial distance from sensor (geometric property of LiDAR) for weighting, not frequency decomposition.

hypothesis: A distance-weighted flow matching objective that upweights gradients from distant points will improve generation quality for sparse far-field regions by 25-35% (measured by Chamfer Distance at 50m+) without degrading near-field fidelity.

confirming: Statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) in far-field Chamfer Distance (50m+) over standard flow matching
No significant degradation in near-field Chamfer Distance (0-20m)
Qualitative improvement in distant structure generation (buildings, vehicles at 50m+)
refuting: If uniform weighting is optimal, validates standard practice and reveals distance-aware weighting is unnecessary
If distance weighting degrades near-field quality, reveals fundamental quality-range trade-off
If learned adaptive weighting underperforms simple inverse distance, suggests geometric heuristics suffice

computational_requirements.data_preparation_time: ~2 hours for KITTI-360 download and preprocessing

novelty_claim: First work to incorporate radial distance awareness into flow matching training for LiDAR point cloud generation. Addresses a fundamental geometric property of LiDAR data that is ignored by standard uniform-weighting approaches. Differentiated from concurrent LiFlow work which addresses conditional scene completion rather than unconditional generation with distance-weighted training.
